_doc: Owner-signed DISPUTED STATEMENT records for THE DRAW real card set (Ticket E0.4).
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS — AND WHY IT IS NOT A RULE. E2's F2 finding is the one class
no rule in this pipeline can reach: a Wikidata statement that RESOLVES perfectly and
is simply FALSE. Every provenance check we have passes on it. E0.2's phantom rule
drops refs that do not resolve; E0.3's live sweep re-checked all 8436 memberships
against Wikidata and found 0 unresolvable refs. Neither sees a lie that is well
formed. E2 said so plainly: the only instrument that found any F2 instance was a
SECOND SOURCE, and all three it found were found by accident.
So this file is not a detector. It is a ledger of instances a human has established
by other means, signed, so that what we know does not live only in a session
transcript — the lesson BUILD_NOTES §11 paid for twice.
verdict 'notCredited' means: the statement is REAL, LIVE and preserved verbatim
below, but it is not credited as provenance for the fact it would otherwise support.
It is deliberately NOT dropped. Dropping it would be worse than keeping it: see the
Dybala record, where the disputed statement is the only thing carrying a value that
is independently CONFIRMED CORRECT.
EFFECT ON THE ARTIFACT. A disputed statement does not change a value — it changes
what we CLAIM about that value. The affected fact is forced to amber in the dossier
with this record attached, so a reader sees the value, the statement behind it, and
the reason the two do not add up. The canonical playersSourced.json is untouched:
a ruling never edits a fact, and cp-assert still proves the file reproduces from the
rules alone.
E0.5 EXTENSION — dropPrintedClub (the Benatia class). Where the disputed statement is
a CLUB the card would print but the player's first-team APPEARANCE there is disputed
(affectsFacts ['clubs']), the record carries dropPrintedClub = <clubQid>. The selector
suppresses that CHIP from the card face and fills the slot from the next club by tenure.
The membership still stays in the canonical AND in the dossier clubs (marked
disputedAppearance), so nothing is edited away — the card simply stops asserting a
first-team appearance a human has disputed.

records.name: Paulo Dybala

statement.verbatimNote: Preserved exactly as Wikidata carries it. Verified LIVE by E0.3's sweep and again on 2026-07-17: the ref resolves to a real, fully described club (multisports club / association football club). Nothing about the statement is malformed — that is the entire point of the F2 class.

records.dispute: Dybala never played for Club Sport Emelec. His real first senior club, Instituto de Córdoba, is absent from Wikidata's P54 for him entirely.
records.note: The committed sourceStartYear 2011 is CORRECT and stays. E2 confirmed it against independent Argentine press (senior debut for Instituto de Córdoba, 12 August 2011) — the value is right only because the phantom club and the real club happen to share a start year. Right value, invented provenance; correct by coincidence. This record exists because that coincidence is not a fact we should be silently trading on: the card claimed green off a statement that is false. It is now amber, with the reason attached. NOT dropped: E0.2 measured that dropping the Emelec statement moves his debut to Palermo 2012 — turning an independently confirmed CORRECT value into a wrong one. Emelec is not printed on the card (his three printed clubs are Juventus, Roma, Palermo by tenure), so the artifact has never asserted he played there.
records.e2Reference: E2 systemicFindings F2, severity UNMEASURABLE — 'Dybala (debut-setting statement places him at Club Sport Emelec, a club he never played for; his real first club is absent from Wikidata entirely; the committed value is right only because the phantom club and the real club share a start year)'. E2 amberList real_0104: 'Value 2011 CONFIRMED correct by independent Argentine press (Instituto de Cordoba, senior debut 12 Aug 2011) — but its provenance is a phantom Wikidata statement... Right value, invented provenance; correct only by coincidence of year.'

records.name: Mehdi Benatia

statement.verbatimNote: Preserved exactly as Wikidata carries it. The club is real and fully described (association football club); the statement resolves. What is disputed is the APPEARANCE, not the entity — the F2-adjacent case where the membership exists in the source but the first-team competitive appearance behind it does not.

records.dispute: ESPN's career history for Benatia omits Marseille ENTIRELY: its earliest season row is 2006/07 at Tours. He was in OM's system without a first-team competitive appearance. The printed Marseille chip (2005-2008) therefore asserted a first-team spell the appearance record does not support.
records.note: The Marseille CHIP is dropped from the card (the ticket's Benatia-class disposition). The membership STAYS in the canonical playersSourced.json as source data — a ruling never edits a fact — and stays in the dossier clubs marked disputedAppearance with this record. Only the chip is suppressed, so the card no longer asserts a first-team appearance a source disputes. The card's other clubs by tenure (Juventus, Bayern, Udinese) fill the three slots. Benatia's sourceStartYear is a SEPARATE within-bucket contradiction (2005 vs ESPN 2006, both era 3) recorded as accepted noise in ownerCitedOverrides — no era override.
records.e2Reference: E2.1 red real_0401: 'debutYear + printedSpells contradicted by non-Wikidata source... COMPOUND: ESPN's earliest season row is 2006/07 at Tours, and its career history omits Marseille ENTIRELY — so the card's printed Marseille spell (2005-2008) is itself questionable, not just the debut year. He was in OM's system without a first-team competitive appearance.'
